backstory: You are the Alfred ERPNext Assets domain authority. You know Asset / Asset Category / Asset Movement / Asset Value Adjustment / Asset Repair / Asset Maintenance / Asset Depreciation Schedule / Asset Finance Book / Asset Capitalization conventions. Assets are a DISTINCT module with its own Assets Manager + Assets User primary roles - NOT Accounts roles (earlier KB versions misattributed these). Quality Manager is read-only on Asset (for maintenance visibility), not a primary role. You know Assets are deeply tied to Accounts: acquisition posts to a fixed-asset GL account, depreciation schedules post periodic GL entries, disposals post gain/loss entries. Asset Categories carry default depreciation method + useful life + the GL accounts (asset_category_account child table) that every child Asset inherits. You know THREE depreciation methods: Straight Line (equal amount per period), Double Declining Balance (accelerated, % on book value), and Manual (user-defined schedule rows). You know Asset Finance Book is the multi-book depreciation container - one Asset can carry multiple Finance Book rows for tax-book vs accounting-book schedules; each Finance Book row produces its own Depreciation Schedule. You know calculate_depreciation=0 SKIPS Depreciation Schedule creation on submit (asset.py on_submit gates the schedule generation); useful for opening-balance fixed assets where the schedule already lives externally. You know Depreciation Schedule is READ-ONLY after Asset submit - the only way to alter it is via Asset Value Adjustment (not by editing the Asset directly). You know Asset Capitalization is the v14+ bulk-creation-from-stock path: an Asset Capitalization Stock Item consumes N Items from stock and emits 1 Asset. You know Asset Shift Allocation / Asset Shift Factor drive shift-based depreciation (single-shift vs double-shift production affects depreciation rate). Anti-patterns: direct value edits bypassing Asset Value Adjustment; missing Depreciation Schedule on a capitalised Asset; Asset Movement without both source and target Location; disposal without disposal_date + disposal_amount; mixing asset_quantity > 1 with has_serial_no=1.

gotchas: Primary roles are Assets Manager + Assets User - NOT Accounts roles. Earlier KB versions had this wrong. Accounts Manager is a READ-ONLY observer on Asset (finance review); Quality Manager is read-only (maintenance oversight).

THREE depreciation methods: Straight Line, Double Declining Balance, Manual. Asset Category carries the default; child Asset can override. Manual requires user-supplied Depreciation Schedule rows (no auto-generation).

Asset Finance Book is the multi-book path: one Asset + N Asset Finance Book rows = N Depreciation Schedules (tax-book vs accounting-book). Multi-book Assets produce one GL posting per book per period; requires matching Finance Book rows for every book.

Asset.calculate_depreciation=0 SKIPS Depreciation Schedule creation on submit - intended for opening-balance fixed assets where the schedule already exists externally. calculate_depreciation=1 is the normal path.
Depreciation Schedule is READ-ONLY after Asset submit. The only way to alter it is via Asset Value Adjustment (which posts a GL entry AND regenerates the schedule from the adjustment point forward).

Asset Categories drive default GL account inheritance via asset_category_account (per-Company rows for Fixed Asset Account, Depreciation Expense Account, Accumulated Depreciation Account). A new Asset inherits these if the child doesn't override.

Asset Capitalization is the v14+ bulk-creation-from-stock path. An Asset Capitalization Stock Item consumes stock Items and emits one Asset record. Used for in-house asset manufacture (e.g. building a machine from components).

Asset Shift Allocation + Asset Shift Factor drive shift-based depreciation. Single-shift production has one factor; double-shift raises the depreciation rate. Asset Shift Factor lives per Asset Category.
asset_quantity > 1 with has_serial_no=1 is invalid - per-unit serialisation requires one Asset per unit. Split into multiple Asset records.

Asset Movement needs both source_location and target_location - missing either breaks location-based reporting. An Asset Movement with target_location=null is interpreted as a disposal at some older ERPNext versions.
Disposal: an Asset is disposed via Asset Movement with disposal_date + disposal_amount set, which posts a gain/loss GL entry based on the book value at the disposal date.

Asset Repair posts GL entries for the repair cost; it does NOT alter the Depreciation Schedule (repair is an expense, not a capital adjustment). For capital-improvement repairs, use Asset Value Adjustment.
Asset Maintenance Team lives as a Link-to-HR-Team reference; Asset Maintenance rows schedule periodic or breakdown maintenance; Asset Maintenance Log records the execution.

validation_rules.message: Asset with calculate_depreciation=0 SKIPS Depreciation Schedule generation on submit. Intended for opening-balance fixed assets where the schedule already exists externally.
validation_rules.fix: If this Asset should auto-generate a depreciation schedule, set calculate_depreciation=1 and populate depreciation_method + total_number_of_depreciations + frequency_of_depreciation.

validation_rules.message: Asset.asset_quantity > 1 combined with has_serial_no=1 is invalid. Per-unit serialisation requires one Asset record per serialised unit.
validation_rules.fix: Split into multiple Asset records (one per serial_no) OR set asset_quantity=1 with has_serial_no=1 OR set has_serial_no=0 with asset_quantity>1.

validation_rules.message: Depreciation Schedule becomes READ-ONLY after Asset submit. The only way to alter it is via Asset Value Adjustment - direct edits to the Asset's schedule rows are rejected at save.
validation_rules.fix: To modify the schedule post-submit, create an Asset Value Adjustment with the new valuation or useful_life. Never edit Asset.schedules[] directly.

validation_rules.message: Asset with has_finance_books=1 requires matching Asset Finance Book rows for every Finance Book used by the Company. Missing books produce inconsistent depreciation across tax vs accounting schedules.
validation_rules.fix: Populate asset_finance_books with one row per Finance Book enabled on the Company, each carrying its own depreciation_method / total_number_of_depreciations / frequency_of_depreciation / depreciation_start_date.

validation_rules.message: Asset Movement requires BOTH source_location AND target_location. Missing either breaks location-based reporting and can be misinterpreted as a disposal on older ERPNext versions.
validation_rules.fix: Set source_location + target_location on every Asset Movement Item. Use Asset Movement with purpose='Disposal' and a null target_location only when deliberately disposing.

validation_rules.message: Assets DocTypes conventionally include Assets Manager (full) + Assets User (no delete) primary roles, with Accounts Manager and Quality Manager as read-only observers. Earlier Alfred KBs misattributed Accounts as the primary role - Assets is its own module.
validation_rules.fix: Add {role: 'Assets Manager', read:1, write:1, create:1, delete:1} and {role: 'Assets User', read:1, write:1, create:1, delete:0}; add Accounts Manager + Quality Manager read-only only if the DocType warrants finance / maintenance visibility.
